name: auto-sklearn (Automated Machine Learning for Scikit-learn)

performance.notes: Optimization time is user-controlled (seconds to hours); per-trial evaluation varies by model

agent_readiness.notes: Bayesian AutoML on top of scikit-learn with meta-learning and ensemble construction. import autosklearn.classification. clf = autosklearn.classification.AutoSklearnClassifier(time_left_for_this_task=120, per_run_time_limit=30). clf.fit(X_train, y_train). clf.predict(X_test). clf.sprint_statistics(). clf.show_models(). Regression: AutoSklearnRegressor. Multi-label: AutoSklearnEstimator. Uses SMAC3 for Bayesian HPO. Ensemble building from top models. Meta-learning: warm-starts from similar datasets. Linux only (SMAC3 dependency). Requires swig and compiled deps.

gotchas: Linux only — does not support macOS or Windows natively
Requires swig and libsvm as system dependencies
SMAC3 dependency adds significant complexity to installation
Memory and time limits must be tuned carefully for large datasets
Stale development — primarily maintained for research
best_when: Research and benchmarking AutoML methods on tabular data
When you want Bayesian HPO with meta-learning warm-starts
Automated ensemble construction from sklearn-compatible models
avoid_when: Production AutoML (use FLAML, AutoGluon, or PyCaret instead)
Deep learning — only sklearn estimators supported
summary: auto-sklearn automates machine learning on tabular data using Bayesian optimization (SMAC3) and meta-learning over 100+ scikit-learn estimators. AutoSklearnClassifier(time_left_for_this_task=120).fit(X, y). Ensemble construction from top models. Meta-learning warm-starts from similar datasets. Linux only — requires swig/libsvm. BSD-3. Research-grade AutoML; for production prefer AutoGluon, FLAML, or PyCaret.
